instructions: Perform a comprehensive audit of the Jitsu codebase to identify architectural violations of DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) and SoC (Separation of Concerns). Specifically: 1) Identify every instance of hardcoded system prompts in cli/main.py and core/executor.py. 2) Map the duplication of LLM client initialization and environment loading across all modules. 3) Locate logic in cli/main.py that violates the Separation of Concerns (e.g., orchestrating planning loops or managing file I/O for epics). 4) Scan for any remaining # noqa or # pragma markers that were not caught in previous cleanups. Output a detailed report in docs/audit_report_v1.md listing specific line numbers, the nature of the violation, and recommended structural fixes.
anti_patterns: Do not modify any source code in this phase.
Do not suggest 'band-aid' fixes; focus on root-cause architectural violations.
Do not ignore violations that are currently covered by tests.
completion_criteria: A comprehensive audit report exists in docs/audit_report_v1.md.
The report identifies all hardcoded prompts and client duplications.
The report outlines a clear separation strategy for the CLI and Core logic.
Repository verification succeeds with just verify.
